title: festuca rubra subsp. duriuscula
intro: Festuca rubra subsp. duriuscula, also known as Scotch moor-grass or hard fescue, is a perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Festuca rubra subsp. duriuscula include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and fighting off infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
